title: URL References, Resolution, Identity Keys, Unicode, and Canonicalization · Program Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for url references, resolution, identity keys, unicode, and canonicalization and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, network, DNS, parser, filesystem, privacy, accessibility, legal-review, scale, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for URL resolution and identity pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case p…
objectives: Parse scheme, authority, userinfo, host, port, path, query, and fragment without treating a URL as a trustworthy string.
Resolve relative, scheme-relative, path-relative, query-only, and fragment-only references against the effective document base.
Remove fragments from network-fetch identity while preserving them when the stakeholder report needs document-location evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Unicode Idna Boundary
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Unicode Idna Boundary: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz site-quality team handles case 4416 during a multilingual public-service site review. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile URL parsing, base resolution, fragment handling, conservative fetch identity, and Unicode host evidence; automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance; then introduce a block, private field, dangerous destination, or spreadsheet formula and stop or reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep display and network host forms distinct, validate Unicode and IDNA conversions, and make confusable or failed conversions visible.
